verbatim_map.@@verbatim:4:2c99ef@@:
<script>

                        var parent = document.currentScript.parentNode;

                        var id = generateId();
                        parent.id = id;

                        MathCell(id, []);

                        parent.update = function (id) {

                            var pi = Math.PI;

                            //Real component ln(sqrt(x x + y y))
                            var Re = (x, y) => Math.log(Math.sqrt(x * x + y * y));

                            var data = [];

                            var s = parametric((u, v) => [u * Math.cos(v),
                            u * Math.sin(v),
                            Re(u * Math.cos(v), u * Math.sin(v))
                            ],
                                [0.0001, 3], [-pi, pi],
                                {
                                    color: '#80aaff',
                                    opacity: 0.9
                                }
                            );


                            data.push(s);

                            var config = {
                                type: 'threejs',
                                frame: true,
                                viewpoint: [1, -1, 1],
                                zMin: -3,
                                zMax: 3
                            };

                            evaluate(id, data, config);

                        }

                        parent.update(id);

                    </script>
verbatim_map.@@verbatim:5:adc984@@:
<script>

                        var parent = document.currentScript.parentNode;

                        var id = generateId();
                        parent.id = id;

                        MathCell(id, []);

                        parent.update = function (id) {

                            var pi = Math.PI;

                            //Imaginary component arctan()
                            var ImB = (x, y) => Math.atan2(y, x) + 2 * pi * 2;
                            var ImA = (x, y) => Math.atan2(y, x) + 2 * pi;
                            var Im0 = (x, y) => Math.atan2(y, x);
                            var Ima = (x, y) => Math.atan2(y, x) - 2 * pi;
                            var Imb = (x, y) => Math.atan2(y, x) - 2 * pi * 2;



                            var data = [];

                            var den = 1000;

                            var sB = parametric((u, v) => [u * Math.cos(v),
                            u * Math.sin(v),
                            ImB(u * Math.cos(v), u * Math.sin(v))
                            ],
                                [0, 2], [-pi, pi - pi / den],
                                {
                                    color: '#cc0066',
                                    opacity: 0.9
                                }
                            );

                            var sA = parametric((u, v) => [u * Math.cos(v),
                            u * Math.sin(v),
                            ImA(u * Math.cos(v), u * Math.sin(v))
                            ],
                                [0, 2], [-pi, pi - pi / den],
                                {
                                    color: '#80aaff',
                                    opacity: 0.9
                                }
                            );
                            var s0 = parametric((u, v) => [u * Math.cos(v),
                            u * Math.sin(v),
                            Im0(u * Math.cos(v), u * Math.sin(v))
                            ],
                                [0, 2], [-pi, pi - pi / den],
                                {
                                    color: '#6666ff',
                                    opacity: 0.9
                                }
                            );

                            var sa = parametric((u, v) => [u * Math.cos(v),
                            u * Math.sin(v),
                            Ima(u * Math.cos(v), u * Math.sin(v))
                            ],
                                [0, 2], [-pi, pi - pi / den],
                                {
                                    color: '#00b3b3',
                                    opacity: 0.9
                                }
                            );

                            var sb = parametric((u, v) => [u * Math.cos(v),
                            u * Math.sin(v),
                            Imb(u * Math.cos(v), u * Math.sin(v))
                            ],
                                [0, 2], [-pi, pi - pi / den],
                                {
                                    color: '#b3b3b3',
                                    opacity: 0.9
                                }
                            );

                            data.push(sB);
                            data.push(sA);
                            data.push(s0);
                            data.push(sa);
                            data.push(sb);

                            var config = {
                                type: 'threejs',
                                frame: true,
                                viewpoint: [1, -1, 0.5],
                                zMin: -16,
                                zMax: 16
                            };

                            evaluate(id, data, config);

                        }

                        parent.update(id);

                    </script>

Branches of logarithms

From definition (\ref{log2}) let @@math:54:997612@@ (@@math:55:bbcbe9@@), so we can write

Now, let @@math:56:bf9db0@@ be any real number. If we restrict the value of @@math:57:8b1443@@ so that $\alpha < \theta < \alpha + 2n\pi$ , then the function
@@math:13:34851b@@
with components @@math:14:e9a92c@@ is a [[em]]single-value[[/em]] and continuous function in the stated domain.

A [[em]]branch[[/em]] of a multiple-valued function @@math:58:6e9921@@ is any single-valued function @@math:59:bf017d@@ that is analytic in some domain at each point @@math:60:6de049@@ of which the value @@math:61:78a0f3@@ is one of the values of @@math:62:27e9e1@@ The requirement of analyticity, of course, prevents @@math:63:609fab@@ from taking on a random selection of the values of @@math:64:b87548@@ Observe that for each fixed @@math:65:f2c4c7@@ the single-valued function (\ref{log3}) is a branch of the multiple-valued function (\ref{log30}). The function
@@math:15:26d978@@
is called the principal branch.

A [[em]]branch cut[[/em]] is a portion of a line or curve that is introduced in order to define a branch
                    @@math:66:6081e6@@ of a multiple-valued function @@math:67:306fc2@@ Points on the branch cut for @@math:68:5d0724@@ are [[em]]singular points[[/em]]
                    of @@math:69:7c2363@@ and any point that is common to all branch cuts of @@math:70:030c22@@ is called a [[em]]branch point[[/em]].
                    The origin and the ray @@math:71:0452b7@@ make up the branch cut for the branch (\ref{log3}) of the
                    logarithmic function. The branch cut for the principal branch (\ref{log4}) consists of the origin
                    and the ray @@math:72:f86d61@@ The origin is evidently a branch point for branches of the multiple-valued
                    logarithmic function.

We can visualize the multiple-valued nature of @@math:73:77b660@@ by
                    using [[a href="http://mathworld.wolfram.com/RiemannSurface.html" target="_blank"]]Riemann
                        surfaces[[/a]].
                    The following interactive images show the real and imaginary

                        identified with a different color.[[/em]]

Special care must be taken in using branches of the logarithmic function, especially since expected
                    identities involving logarithms do not always carry over from calculus.
